message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: I need to modify Features.update() in features.py to add deep-merge behavior. What file(s) does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in features.py to sync _column_requires_decoding dict. Please propose your files, disjoint from this one if possible.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py so can't split by file. Let's split by region instead: I will ONLY touch the body of Features.update() (adding deep-merge logic for dict values in iterable and kwds args) - no other methods, no new decorators, no changes to __init__ or _column_requires_decoding. Please keep your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator and its application out of the update() method body if possible, or if you must decorate update(), do it via @keep_features_dicts_synced applied at the def line only (one line above 'def update') so our edits don't collide on the body. Let me know which methods you'll decorate/touch so I can avoid them. I'll wait for your ACCEPT before editing.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py - can't fully separate files. Let's split by method/region instead: I will add a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator and apply it ONLY as a decorator line above __setitem__, __delitem__, update, and other dict-mutating methods (e.g. __ior__, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear) -- I will NOT touch the internal body/logic of update(), only add a decorator line above its signature. You can freely modify the internal merge logic inside update()'s body for your deep-merge feature. This way our diffs touch different lines. Agreed?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() method only)
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (decorator lines above __setitem__, __delitem__, update, __ior__, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear signatures; new decorator definition)
note: Agreed. I will only touch the body/logic inside update() (not its decorator line or signature). Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Agreed. I will: (1) add a module-level keep_features_dicts_synced decorator function above class Features, (2) apply it via one decorator line above __setitem__, __delitem__, and update (no change to update's body/signature), (3) add new override methods pop, popitem, clear, setdefault, __ior__ (currently NOT overridden in the class - they bypass __setitem__/__delitem__ per CPython dict internals) each decorated with @keep_features_dicts_synced, calling super(). These are new methods inserted right after __delitem__/before update, so should not collide with your update() body edits. I will not touch update()'s body/internals. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Finished my part: added keep_features_dicts_synced decorator (module-level, above class Features) and applied it to __setitem__, __delitem__, update (decorator line only, did not touch update's body), clear, pop, popitem, setdefault (new overrides). Ran tests/features/test_features.py - all 25 passed (3 skipped). Your update() body is untouched by me - go ahead with your deep-merge logic inside it.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py (body of update() method only)
note: Implemented deep-merge in Features.update(): recursively merges nested dict/Sequence(dict)-valued keys instead of overwriting, applied uniformly to both the positional iterable and kwds. Only the body of update() was touched (its def line/signature and everything outside update() untouched), so it should compose cleanly with your decorator lines and new methods. Patch written to patch.txt. Done on my end.
